steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $w \left(15w+8\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of 15w\u00b2 and 8w: both terms share a factor of w.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Verify by distributing: w \u00b7 15w = 15w\u00b2 and w \u00b7 8 = 8w. Sum is 15w\u00b2 + 8w. \u2713

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $w \left(15w+8\right)$: Distributing gives 15w\u00b2 + 8w, which matches the original expression exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — $8w \left(15w+1\right)$: Distributing gives 120w\u00b2 + 8w, which does not equal 15w\u00b2 + 8w. ✗
  • Choice C — $15{w}^{2} \left(8w+1\right)$: Distributing gives 120w\u00b3 + 15w\u00b2, which changes both the degree and the coefficients. ✗
  • Choice D — $23 \left({w}^{2}+w\right)$: Distributing gives 23w\u00b2 + 23w, which does not match; 23 is not a factor of 15w\u00b2 + 8w. ✗
A is correct.
